title: Make slirp MTU configurable (network_cmd_options)
body:
The mtu default value is currently forced to 65520.
This let the user control it using the config key network_cmd_options,
i.e.: network_cmd_options=["mtu=9000"]

Closes #8834

Also apply the following diff to add shell completion for that option:
```diff
diff --git a/cmd/podman/common/completion.go b/cmd/podman/common/completion.go
index c9a3c5e94..09dd74e20 100644
--- a/cmd/podman/common/completion.go
+++ b/cmd/podman/common/completion.go
@@ -817,6 +817,7 @@ func AutocompleteNetworkFlag(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string, toComplete strin
                                "allow_host_loopback=": getBoolCompletion,
                                "cidr=":                nil,
                                "enable_ipv6=":         getBoolCompletion,
+                               "mtu=":                 nil,
                                "outbound_addr=":       nil,
                                "outbound_addr6=":      nil,
                                "port_handler=": func(_ string) ([]string, cobra.ShellCompDirective) {
```
Please update man pages with this information.

> Please update man pages with this information.

Searching for relevant man page(s) - I can't seem to find anything I could update. I would need some assistance on this.

@bitstrings The man page for podman create https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/docs/source/markdown/podman-create.1.md#--networkmode---net. You should add the slirp4netns options there. The same has to be done for podman run and podman pod create.
